mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeds 0.5 percentage points, signaling immediate labor market deterioration that triggers negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and housing demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: An index of industrial production above its 12-month moving average confirms sustained manufacturing resilience, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: Industrial production above trend fails to generate positive 20-day returns during expansion regimes, with out-of-sample accuracy below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4 percent forces markets to price in delayed monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation prints above 0.4 percent are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains aggregate income growth, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict 60-day return direction with a Rank IC above 0.02 across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP coinciding with a monthly decline in retail sales confirms a broad-based demand shock, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined GDP contraction and retail sales decline do not yield negative 20-day returns in at least 55 percent of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production increase exceeding 0.3 percent signals accelerating supply-side momentum that translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth above 0.3 percent fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04.
